metallurgical_logic: The melt pool may be too small or unstable to create full interlayer bonding. Lack of fusion, unmelted zones, interlaminar cracks or discontinuous tracks dominate over functional transformation design.
expected_observation: Cracks, lack-of-fusion pores, poor continuity, low usable density. DSC or stress-strain data may be meaningless if specimens cannot be mechanically tested.
functional_consequence: High risk. Shape-memory or superelastic response cannot be trusted until continuity and density are solved.
example_case: Xiang2024 D1: 80 W, 1000 mm/s, 53.3 J/mm³, macroscopic interlaminar cracks, not mechanically tested.

metallurgical_logic: Energy is sufficient for bonding but not so high that Ni evaporation strongly shifts composition. Austenite can remain dominant near room temperature; precipitates and residual defects still matter.
expected_observation: Mostly good forming quality. Some samples may show Ti3Ni/Ni4Ti3 or small defects, but functional recovery can remain strong.
functional_consequence: Best screening candidate for room-temperature superelasticity in Ni-rich LPBF NiTi. Verify by DSC and cyclic compression/tension.
example_case: Xiang2024: B1-B2, C1-C4, D2-D4; many cases show >5.5% initial recovery and >5.0% after 10 cycles.

metallurgical_logic: Higher energy improves melting but can start keyhole-type instability, local Ni evaporation and precipitate changes. The material may still work, but functional recovery becomes less stable.
expected_observation: Keyhole defects or mixed microstructure can appear; measured Ni may begin to decrease; transformation temperature can move toward service range or above it depending on composition.
functional_consequence: Conditional. Can be useful only if density, Ni content and DSC window are measured and acceptable.
example_case: Xiang2024 A1/B3/B4 show partial superelasticity or reduced recovery.

metallurgical_logic: Large energy input increases Ni evaporation. Because NiTi transformation temperatures are extremely composition-sensitive, Ni loss can raise Ms/Af and make the part martensitic at room temperature.
expected_observation: Keyhole pores, oxide coloration, Ti-rich secondary phases or Ni4Ti3/Ti2Ni-related phases may appear depending on local chemistry.
functional_consequence: Poor superelasticity risk. The part may behave more like martensitic NiTi at service temperature.
example_case: Xiang2024 A2-A4: 166.7-233.3 J/mm³, almost no superelasticity; A4 reported lowest transformation critical stress.

metallurgical_logic: The strongest LPBF NiTi strategy is not only high density. It requires coupled control of porosity/cracks, Ni evaporation, oxygen level and transformation temperature.
expected_observation: Defect-free or nearly defect-free structure, controlled chemistry shift, low crack density and stable superelastic response.
functional_consequence: Can reach exceptional tensile superelasticity if chemistry and oxygen are controlled.
example_case: Xue2022 Acta Materialia reports as-printed room-temperature tensile superelasticity up to 6% by eliminating porosity/cracks, tailoring Ni evaporation and controlling oxygen.

scenario: Energy density is not a sufficient descriptor
metallurgical_logic: Different combinations of power, speed, hatch and layer thickness can have the same VED but different melt-pool lifetime, thermal gradient, evaporation rate, cooling rate and texture.
expected_observation: Samples with similar VED may show different phase transformation temperatures, density or microstructure.
functional_consequence: ML and process maps should keep P, v, h and t as separate features, not only VED.
example_case: Large power-speed studies and 2024/2026 reports emphasize nonlinear influence of laser power and scan speed on transformation behavior.

metallurgical_logic: ML is useful only when the database stores process variables, composition, heat treatment and functional outputs separately. It should not train on VED alone.
expected_observation: Model can predict density, UTS, elongation, hysteresis or Af when enough exact rows exist.
functional_consequence: Use Gaussian Process for small datasets; tree models for larger datasets; always report whether demo rows were excluded.
example_case: Li2024 uses 195 entries from 23 publications for multi-output LPBF NiTi optimization; another 2024 study reports GRNN R² = 0.97 for transition-temperature prediction.
